title: frangula californica subsp. tomentella
intro: Frangula californica subsp. tomentella, also known as California buckthorn, is a plant species native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anthraquinone content, which has been shown to have laxative and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Frangula californica subsp. tomentella have been used to treat various health issues, including constipation, diarrhea, and skin conditions, due to its ability to stimulate bowel movements and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
